etymology.description: 世(世) (shì, "generation; life; era") refers to the succession of human generations or life stages, encompassing time and overall human existence. 界(界) (jiè, "boundary; sphere") represents the limits or divisions between different entities or areas, leading to a conception of different realms or domains. Together, they form the idea of the "world" as a sphere consisting of all generations and realms, encompassing all of humankind and the physical domain around them.
etymology.interpretation: 世(世) (shì, "generation; life; era") emphasizes continuity and the passage of time across human generations, implying a collective human existence over eras. 界(界) (jiè, "boundary; sphere") is about demarcation, defining spaces or worlds in philosophical, physical, or existential terms. Thus, 世界 (shìjiè) embodies the concept of the world as the overarching domain that includes all human societies and the physical universe in which they exist.

examples.chinese: 我们住在一个美丽的世界(我們住在一個美麗的世界)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhù zài yī gè měilì de shìjiè.
examples.english: We live in a beautiful world.

examples.chinese: 世界上有很多不同的文化(世界上有很多不同的文化)。
examples.pinyin: Shìjiè shàng yǒu hěn duō bùtóng de wénhuà.
examples.english: There are many different cultures in the world.

examples.chinese: 科学家在探索新的世界(科學家在探索新的世界)。
examples.pinyin: Kēxuéjiā zài tànsuǒ xīn de shìjiè.
